steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: The verb after the blank must agree with the subject "re-entering one of the checkers into the game." That whole gerund phrase acts as a singular subject, and the sentence describes a general rule, so the verb should be simple present singular.

steps.title: Rule out mismatched verbs
steps.content: B and C use plural auxiliaries, so they do not agree with the singular subject. D is also a plural/base form. The singular simple-present form matches both the subject and the sentence's general-rule context. The answer is A.
